authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(107) — Pine Creek (Goose Lake Tributary) and tributaries (Modoc Co.)
sources.quotedText: Pine Creek (Goose Lake Tributary) and tributaries (Modoc Co.). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. 5 trout

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 264748, "Pine Creek", Stream, Modoc Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":264748,"gaz_name":"Pine Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Modoc","objectid_1":1411974}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13417365.6225, 5079342.3106) converts to lon -120.530246, lat 41.452676, near Alturas and immediately adjacent to Pine Creek Reservoir (GNIS gaz_id 264752, lon -120.422967, lat 41.423332), which this creek feeds; corrects prior file coordinates (-120.2972, 41.9927), which were guessed near Goose Lake itself and were off by roughly 61 km from this GNIS-recorded stream location.

parameters.note: Applies to Pine Creek (Goose Lake tributary) and its tributaries.

regulations.humanSummary: Open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last day in February
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag limit; this special regulation entry does not specify a distinct possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
